papers.title_en: Image-Conditioned Instance Prompt Network for Referring Remote Sensing Image Segmentation
papers.authors: Biaoyu Ren, Qingsheng Wang, Cun Xu, Dingkang Yang, Wenxuan Wang
papers.evidence: 遥感图像指代分割中的跨模态特征融合
papers.tldr: 遥感图像指代分割要求模型精准定位语言描述的目标，但现有方法因文本描述粒度不足和语义偏移导致跨模态融合瓶颈。本文提出ICIPNet，通过图像条件实例提示模块生成自适应视觉语义表示，并利用双边信息融合从通道和令牌维度增强特征融合。在多个遥感数据集上，ICIPNet取得了优于现有方法的性能。该工作为遥感指代分割提供了有效的动态提示与双向融合方案。
papers.abstract_en: Referring Remote Sensing Image Segmentation (RRSIS) is a situated, task-driven cross-modal task related to the embodied perception paradigm, requiring models to align visual-spatial features with linguistic intentions for precise target perception. Recent research has focused on refining the granularity of textual features and optimizing image-text feature fusion to better guide target feature representations. However, insufficient descriptive granularity and sensitivity to semantic shifts can cause bottlenecks in cross-modal feature fusion. To address these issues, we propose the Image-Conditioned Instance Prompt Network (ICIPNet) with Bilateral Information Fusion, which is designed to alleviate bottlenecks in cross-modal feature fusion. ICIPNet introduces an Image-Conditioned Instance Prompt (ICIP) module to generate self-adaptive visual and semantic representations without external knowledge. The Bilateral Information Fusion (BIF) module enhances feature fusion along the token and channel dimensions. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed ICIPNet outperforms existing RRSIS models.

papers.title_en: Towards Unified Vision-Language Models with Incomplete Multi-Modal Inputs
papers.authors: Xiang Fang, Wanlong Fang, Changshuo Wang, Keke Tang, Daizong Liu, Siyi Wang, Wei Ji
papers.evidence: 视频语言模型中不完整多模态输入的统一框架
papers.tldr: 现有视频语言模型假设输入完整，但真实场景中摄像头失效或数据隐私保护常导致模态缺失，造成训练与测试分布不一致，影响模型的可靠性与安全性。本文首次提出统一的视觉语言模型框架，针对不完整多模态输入场景设计即插即用模块，无需重新训练即可适配现有模型。实验表明该方法在缺失模态的任务上显著提升性能，为实际部署提供了鲁棒解决方案。
papers.abstract_en: Video-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive multi-modal reasoning capabilities across diverse computer vision applications. However, these VLMs are task-specific and assume that both video and language inputs are complete. However, real-world VLM applications might face challenges due to deactivated sensors (e.g., cameras are unavailable due to data privacy), yielding modality-incomplete data and leading to inconsistency between training and testing data. While straightforward incomplete input can boast training generalization-ability and lead to training failure, its potential risks to VLMs regarding safety and trustworthiness have been largely neglected. To this end, we make the first attempt to propose a unified incomplete video-language model to process the incomplete multi-modal inputs. Extensive experimental results show that our method can serve as a plug-and-play module for previous works to improve their performance in various multi-modal tasks.
